title: Intensely Juicy Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A nostalgic Saudi Arabian dish featuring fatty and juicy lamb, this recipe is a must try for anyone looking for a flavorful and aromatic meal. The use of mustard powder and katsuramuki-cut lamb makes this dish truly unique.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, making sure it reaches a safe internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together mustard powder, salt, black pepper, cumin powder, and coriander powder.
Add the katsuramuki-cut lamb to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Thread the lamb onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the lamb with lemon juice and olive oil.
Grill the lamb for 8-10 minutes per side, or until it reaches a safe internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the lamb rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, Saudi Arabian lamb kebabs are served with a side of rice or flatbread.
To make the dish more substantial, serve with a side of roasted vegetables or a salad.
